series.title: DMS Plan
series.subtitle: Data management, workflow design, and repository delivery
series.description: A series for operational DMS planning, data workflow design, and the path from project systems to repository-ready delivery.

entries.title: Data Management and Sharing Plan
entries.subtitle: From project systems to repository delivery
entries.summary: An operational workflow presentation showing how DMS planning becomes real work from source systems to structured data and repository delivery, with a live Builder-plus-review service that helps teams start, update, and review plans.

series.title: Formal SignWriting
series.subtitle: Plain-text encodings, grammar, search, rendering, styling, and implementation of written sign text
series.description: A technical publication series on the Formal SignWriting model for sign text that can be stored, searched, checked, and processed by software, with a clear distinction between canonical text and presentation markup.

entries.title: Formal SignWriting
entries.subtitle: The two-part word, sign text, and the core model
entries.summary: Canonical opening statement of the two-part word and of sign text that software can store, search, check, and process.

entries.title: FSW and SWU
entries.subtitle: Plain-text encodings, character sets, and conversion
entries.summary: Plain-text encodings, character sets, conversion, and canonical text boundaries.

entries.title: The Shape of a Sign
entries.subtitle: Symbols, coordinates, centering, and the signbox
entries.summary: Canonical statement of symbols, coordinates, centering, and signbox structure.

entries.title: Grammar of Formal SignWriting
entries.subtitle: Token patterns, syntax, and valid structure
entries.summary: Token patterns, syntax, regular expressions, and well-formed Formal SignWriting text.

entries.title: Searching Signed Text
entries.subtitle: Query language, matching, ranges, variance, and search logic
entries.summary: Canonical statement of query language, matching, ranges, variance, and signed-text search.

entries.title: Rendering Formal SignWriting
entries.subtitle: SVG, fonts, layout, and output models
entries.summary: Canonical statement of SVG, fonts, layout, and output models kept distinct from canonical text.

entries.title: Styling Signs
entries.subtitle: The style string as light markup for customization and rich presentation
entries.summary: The style string as optional light markup for customization and rich presentation.

entries.title: Formal SignWriting in Practice
entries.subtitle: Libraries, datasets, transformations, and implementation pathways
entries.summary: Canonical statement of libraries, datasets, transformations, and real implementation pathways.

series.title: Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure Initiative
series.subtitle: Infrastructure, partnership, and sustainability materials
series.description: A series for collaboration, institutional support, international partnership, and long-term SignWriting infrastructure framing.

entries.title: Executive Brief
entries.subtitle: Sutton SignWriting as research infrastructure
entries.summary: A concise forwardable presentation that states the mission, support gap, and opportunity around Sutton SignWriting.

entries.title: Initiative Overview
entries.subtitle: What already exists, why it matters now, and where collaboration fits
entries.summary: A collaboration-facing overview of the program lanes, evidence base, and institutional value around Sutton SignWriting.

entries.title: Infrastructure and Stewardship
entries.subtitle: Confirmed assets, current risks, and the case for stronger institutional support
entries.summary: A presentation showing Sutton SignWriting as existing infrastructure with real assets, active maintenance, and identifiable support risks.

entries.title: Brazil as the Empirical Center of SignWriting
entries.subtitle: Documented institutional density, research opportunity, and why Brazil matters
entries.summary: A compact evidence brief showing why Brazil is the strongest documented empirical center for SignWriting use, instruction, and future research.

entries.title: South Korea as a Current Institutional Proof Point
entries.subtitle: National dictionary publication, current institutional use, and why Korea matters now
entries.summary: A compact evidence brief showing that SignWriting is already present inside a public Korean Sign Language dictionary published by the National Institute of Korean Language.

entries.title: Research Program for Literacy Without Sound
entries.subtitle: What could be studied next, why Brazil matters, and what support would make it possible
entries.summary: A practical brief that turns the science framing into a buildable SignWriting research program without turning the project into a comparison contest.

entries.title: Other Sign Language Scripts and Their Conceptions
entries.subtitle: ASL-phabet, HamNoSys, ASLwrite, and what they imply about written sign language
entries.summary: A concise infrastructure-facing presentation naming a few prominent sign-language script conceptions without turning them into the center of the SignWriting case.

entries.title: International Shared Support
entries.subtitle: How international partners can help keep the system stable and connected
entries.summary: An international support model combining University of Iowa backing, partner relationships, and country ambassadors.

entries.title: Country Ambassadors
entries.subtitle: Country-level outreach, translation, and local relationship-building
entries.summary: A volunteer-first country ambassador model for trusted local outreach, translation, and early relationship-building.

series.title: The Science of Sutton SignWriting
series.subtitle: Language, literacy, brain pathways, and SignWriting as research infrastructure
series.description: A research series on the science of signed language, written sign, literacy, and the infrastructure needed to study them well.

entries.title: Language Without Sound
entries.subtitle: Brain networks, pathway expansion, literacy, and the case for SignWriting infrastructure
entries.summary: Presentation 1 in The Science of Sutton SignWriting series.

entries.title: The Shape of Writing
entries.subtitle: Why signed writing does not need to fit into a line
entries.summary: Writing is not inherently linear. This second science entry explains why SignWriting should be understood as plane-based writing rather than as a line script with unusual symbols.

series.title: Unicode and SignWriting
series.subtitle: Compatibility, chronology, and direct-link technical notes
series.description: A hidden document library for Unicode history, compatibility, Wikimedia context, developer guidance, and UTC-facing notes.

entries.title: Unicode Overview
entries.subtitle: Current position and why the issue keeps returning
entries.summary: Short baseline explanation of why official Unicode SignWriting is not sufficient for the Sutton SignWriting production ecosystem.

entries.title: Developer Notes
entries.subtitle: What to use now and what not to assume
entries.summary: Implementation guidance for developers working with Sutton SignWriting data, conversion, fonts, search, and Unicode-facing workflows.

entries.title: Chronology and Record
entries.subtitle: Official submissions, minutes, and related documents
entries.summary: A dated record of the main Unicode and related SignWriting documents relevant to current compatibility and standards discussions.

entries.title: Wikimedia Brief
entries.subtitle: What Wikimedia needs to understand about Unicode and SignWriting
entries.summary: A short practical note for Wikimedia staff and collaborators about the current Unicode gap, current workarounds, and possible next steps.

entries.title: UTC Technical Note
entries.subtitle: Draft note on compatibility, spatial composition, and next steps
entries.summary: A formal draft note stating that current official Unicode SignWriting is not sufficient for compatible Sutton SignWriting production use.

series.title: University of Iowa

entries.summary: A short University of Iowa-facing routing memo for internal forwarding, summarizing the opportunity and directing readers to the right next conversation.

entries.title: Lab Home
entries.subtitle: A practical path from 0 percent placement to grants and infrastructure

entries.title: Steve Slevinski Value
entries.subtitle: Practical DMS, repository, and research workflow value for the University of Iowa
entries.summary: A University of Iowa-facing presentation focused on Steve Slevinski's immediate operational value through DMS planning, workflow design, code, data shaping, and repository delivery.

entries.title: Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure Initiative
entries.subtitle: A path to shared research infrastructure at the University of Iowa
entries.summary: A University of Iowa-facing presentation about turning Sutton SignWriting from fragile independent maintenance into durable shared research infrastructure.

entries.title: Infrastructure Core and Philanthropy
entries.subtitle: A University of Iowa support structure for global SignWriting infrastructure
entries.summary: A University of Iowa-facing presentation framing the Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure Core as a philanthropic opportunity that complements the lab-home, operational-value, and grant-development structure.

entries.title: Relevant Precedents and Institutional Pathways
entries.subtitle: What comparable university models suggest for SignWriting at Iowa
entries.summary: A University of Iowa-facing supporting presentation using language, archive, sign-language, and data-infrastructure precedents to show that SignWriting follows a recognizable institutional path rather than requiring a speculative leap.
